verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: “Again we must ask,” he says, “what sin is,—natural? or accidental?

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: If natural, it is not sin; if accidental, it is separable; and if it is separable, it can be avoided; and because it can be avoided, man can be without that which can be avoided.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: The answer to this is, that sin is not natural; but nature (especially in that corrupt state from which we have become by nature “children of wrath”) has too little determination of will to avoid sin, unless assisted and healed by God’s grace through Jesus Christ our Lord.
